plang.note: Worst clean-binary run. Flaps 0-4 fail across identical runs; see finding 1.

coverage.note: F1-F5 production files are covered by the C# suite (3815 green). The defect is PLang fixture reproducibility, not C# line coverage.

findings.issue: plang --test is not reproducibly green from a clean binary. Five+ clean runs gave 4, 0, 1, 0 failures (and a back-to-back run went 0 then 1). Committed .pr fixtures encode variable.set's Type parameter in the PRE-stage-4 bare-string shape ({"value":"image","type":"string"}); stage 4 (coder v4) changed Type to a structured {name,kind,strict} entity. The runtime runs the stale .pr wrong (ReadPhotoStampsImage: runtime error 'Cannot convert app.type.image.this to String') or LLM-rebuilds it on the fly. The builder is non-deterministic (it logs 'step count didn't match ... the LLM is non-deterministic') and the builder LLM cache (Tests/.db/system.sqlite) is gitignored, so a fresh clone is cold and flaps. 688/703 committed .pr are still old-format; only 15 (the TypeKindStrict goals) are new-format.
findings.impact: A fresh reviewer / CI cannot get a reliable green PLang suite. The coder's and codeanalyzer's '262/262, 0 fail' is a warm-cache artifact, not the committed clean-checkout state. Real type-semantics behavior on this branch (image/text/number kind stamping) is verified only when the LLM happens to rebuild correctly.
findings.suggestion: Run `plang build` to regenerate the whole Tests/ tree against the current binary and COMMIT the refreshed .pr so they match the stage-4 Type entity shape. Then prove it: clean-binary `plang --test` green across >=2 consecutive runs with `git status` clean after each (zero .pr modified by the run).

findings.issue: No end-to-end PLang test walks set -> store -> retrieve -> load -> throw for a LAZY strict image. SetAsImageGifStrictMismatch sets `"photo.png" as image/gif strict` but only asserts Type.Name/Kind; it never forces a byte load, so the deferred StrictKindMismatchException never fires in PLang. Imprint-survival across storage is established only by code-read of Variables.Set's by-reference aliasing (codeanalyzer residual #2).
findings.impact: If a future refactor clones Data on Set (breaking the by-reference imprint), or drops the imprint at the variable.set seam for path-backed values, no PLang test would catch it; the throw-at-load is unit-tested on image directly, bypassing the set seam.
findings.suggestion: Once fixtures are stable, add a goal that reads `%img.Width%` or `%img.Bytes%` after a mismatched strict set on the committed photo.png fixture and asserts the error surfaces (or use test.report error-capture).

summary: C# suite is solid and deterministic (3815/3815); the F1 strict-kind fix that failed v1 is now honestly covered (two distinct enforcement-path tests, builder .pr faithful, codeanalyzer mutation-verified, my code-read confirms). F2-F5 confirmed. BUT the PLang suite is not reproducibly green from a clean binary: committed .pr fixtures are stale w.r.t. the branch's own stage-4 variable.set.Type entity change (688/703 still bare-string format), so the runtime runs them wrong at runtime or LLM-rebuilds them non-deterministically (cache gitignored, cold on fresh clone). Observed 0-4 flapping failures across identical clean runs. FAIL until the Tests/ tree is rebuilt, committed, and shown green over >=2 consecutive clean runs.
